skill.name: gws-meet
skill.description: Manage Google Meet conferences using the gws CLI tool. Create meeting spaces, retrieve conference records, manage participants, and access recordings and transcripts.
skill.summary: Google Meet conference management via gws CLI
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 27 potential issues; all were evaluated as false positives. The file contains markdown documentation only. External command flags were triggered by bash code blocks (not executable code). Network flags were legitimate Google documentation URLs. Filesystem flag was a standard relative path reference. The skill interfaces with Google Meet APIs via the gws CLI tool.

content.user_title: Manage Google Meet conferences with gws CLI
content.value_statement: Need to create meeting spaces, retrieve conference records, or manage Meet participants? This skill provides Claude Code with commands to interact with Google Meet via the gws CLI tool.
actual_capabilities: Create meeting spaces for new Google Meet conferences
List and retrieve conference records by ID
Get details about meeting spaces including active conferences
Access recordings, transcripts, and smart notes
Browse and inspect API resources and methods using gws schema
limitations: Requires the gws CLI tool to be installed and configured
Requires Google Workspace authentication via gws-shared auth rules
Cannot create Meet meetings directly without proper Google Workspace permissions

use_cases.title: Create and manage meeting spaces
use_cases.description: Set up new meeting spaces for scheduled calls or ongoing video conferencing needs.

use_cases.title: Retrieve conference records
use_cases.description: Find past meeting records, access recordings, and pull transcripts for compliance or review.
use_cases.target_user: Compliance officers or team leads reviewing past meetings

use_cases.title: Explore Meet API capabilities
use_cases.description: Browse available API resources and methods to understand what Meet operations are possible.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new meeting space
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Google Meet space for our team meeting using gws meet spaces create
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to set up a meeting room in Google Meet

prompt_templates.title: List recent conferences
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the last 5 Google Meet conference records using gws meet conferenceRecords list

prompt_templates.title: Get meeting space details
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the details of meeting space [SPACE_ID]? Use gws meet spaces get
prompt_templates.scenario: When retrieving information about a specific meeting space

prompt_templates.title: End active conference
prompt_templates.prompt: End any active conference on space [SPACE_ID] using gws meet spaces endActiveConference

output_examples.input: Create a meeting space for the design review
output_examples.output:
Meeting space created successfully with ID: abc123xyz
URL: https://meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij
Join via browser or calendar invite

Recent conferences:
1. Design Review - Started: 2024-01-15 09:00 (Ended: 09:45)
2. Sprint Planning - Started: 2024-01-14 14:00 (Ended: 15:30)
3. Team Standup - Started: 2024-01-14 09:00 (Ended: 09:15)

best_practices: Inspect API methods with gws meet --help before running commands to understand available options
Use gws schema meet.<resource>.<method> to review required parameters and types before making API calls
Store meeting space IDs for later reference when managing specific conferences
anti_patterns: Do not assume the gws CLI is installed without checking prerequisites first
Avoid hardcoding conference IDs without verifying they still exist
Do not skip authentication setup - proper Google Workspace auth is required

faq.question: What prerequisites are needed to use this skill?
faq.answer: The gws CLI tool must be installed. Authentication must be configured via the gws-shared module following the auth setup instructions.

faq.question: Can I create a Google Meet link directly?
faq.answer: Yes, use gws meet spaces create to generate a new meeting space with a unique Meet URL.

faq.question: How do I find past meeting recordings?
faq.answer: Use gws meet conferenceRecords list to find conferences, then gws meet conferenceRecords recordings to retrieve recording data.

faq.question: What is the difference between spaces and conferenceRecords?
faq.answer: Spaces are meeting rooms where conferences happen. ConferenceRecords are the history of meetings including participants, recordings, and transcripts.

faq.question: Can I end a meeting remotely?
faq.answer: Yes, use gws meet spaces endActiveConference on a space that has an active conference.

faq.question: How do I explore all available commands?
faq.answer: Run gws meet --help to see all resources and methods, then use gws schema meet.<resource>.<method> to inspect specific operations.
